description: Lecture 6 introduces the partially synchronous model, a crucial framework for understanding network communication in blockchain systems. It highlights the importance of this model as a balance between synchronous and asynchronous models, discussing its implications for consensus protocols and limitations when dealing with Byzantine nodes.
topic_summary: This lecture covers the partially synchronous model, its significance in blockchain communication, limitations regarding Byzantine nodes, and the CAP theorem's relevance to consensus protocols. Key points include the 33% threshold for Byzantine nodes and the trade-offs in achieving consistency, availability, and partition tolerance.
